sourceDesc.p: Diwnloaded from https://medium.com/@louBurnard/back-to-bennsville-79e291119e54 and translated from HTML to TEI

body.p: At the top of Blackboy Hill is a spot where my friend Chris and I used to sit and mull over our Saturday night successes or failures, most weekends of 1963 or 4. Conveniently located mid-way between my home in Cotham and his in Stoke Bishop, we called it Bennsville in honour of Antony Wedgwood Benn who had recently renounced his title in order to continue as Bristol’s MP. There were (I think) two benches and a street lamp, a roundabout, and lots of traffic. Behind it was a seedy night club called the Glen which we did not patronise. There is much more traffic now and new buildings have appeared : the Glen has been redeveloped as a hospital and Mr AXA has erected an unlovely office block which spoils the view down the hill. Chris died years ago, but one of the benches is still there. So I sat on it and took this photo and thought of him this afternoon after a muddy walk across the downs.
